title: ‘Excited but Shocked’: U.S. Soccer Fans Struggle With Trump’s FIFA Call
summary: Americans wrestled with definitions of fairness, after the president intervened in the World Cup to reinstate a star player.

title: Trump’s Intervention in Balogun’s World Cup Suspension Adds to Conflict With Europe
summary: The decision to reverse a one-game ban landed badly in Europe, at a moment of already-heightened geopolitical tension with the United States.

title: Why A.I. Distillation Has Become a Hot Topic in the Race with China
summary: U.S. companies complain that competitors in China are unfairly copying their A.I. systems using a technique that has been around for years.

title: Alibaba’s A.I. Is a Hit, but Hard to Turn Into a Moneymaker
summary: The Chinese company’s models have won over developers worldwide, but they are open source — so they can be used and modified freely.

title: Michigan Senate Primary Gives Democrats Stark Choice Between Progressive and Moderate Candidate
summary: The state’s important Senate primary just became a head-to-head battle between Abdul El-Sayed, an outspoken left-wing candidate, and Haley Stevens, a moderate backed by party leaders.

title: In Trump’s Second Midterm, Democrats Are Looking for Fighters
summary: Attitudes in the party have shifted, Democrats say, with a growing appetite for outsider candidates vowing to overhaul the system. Some worry that the trend could be costly in November.

title: Bill Frist Wants to Take Politics Out of Climate Change
summary: Bill Frist, a surgeon who also served as Senate majority leader, has emerged as an outspoken advocate for environmental health. “A healthier planet means healthier people,” he said.

title: As Trump Cools on NATO, Turkey Hosts Summit Meeting
summary: As President Trump cools on the alliance, its members see Turkey’s large military and vibrant defense sector as assets.

title: Russia Strikes Kyiv, Ukraine’s Capital, on Eve of NATO Summit
summary: Ballistic missiles were fired into the capital of Ukraine as Russia launched its second major attack in less than a week. At least 12 people were killed, officials said.

title: Ukraine Remade Air Defense, but Russia Has Changed Its Attacks
summary: Ukraine has changed how militaries use Patriot systems. But recent attacks prove that clever adaptations cannot overcome a shortage of interceptors.

title: Turkey Hosts NATO Summit While Cracking Down on Critics
summary: In the weeks leading up to the two-day event, the Turkish authorities blocked websites, jailed scores of people over allegations of terrorism and arrested a prominent comedian.

title: Nancy Ward, the Cherokee Leader, Stokes Division Even 250 Years Later
summary: In 1776, Cherokee leaders looked for ways to navigate a foreboding future. Nanyehi, a.k.a. Nancy Ward, is venerated and reviled for her choice.

title: Efforts to Help Smokers Quit Stall Under Trump
summary: The C.D.C.’s tobacco control office has been shut for more than a year. After its most prominent antismoking campaign went off the air, calls to 1-800-QUIT-NOW lines have plummeted.

title: Zyn’s Popularity Has Tobacco Companies Racing to Cash In
summary: The makers of nicotine pouches are building new plants and expanding to meet demand. While influencers claim health benefits, experts warn the products can be highly addictive.

title: An Email. A Knock at the Door. A Suit Alleges a Threat to Free Speech.
summary: A man sent a scathing note to an immigration official after two demonstrators were killed. A lawsuit says agents who came to his house were trying to intimidate him.

title: For Families With Nonspeaking Autistic Children, a Fierce Debate Over Assisted Spelling
summary: Popular communication methods for nonspeaking autistic people have ignited a fierce debate over what counts as evidence of hidden cognitive abilities.

title: NYC and New Jersey Brace for Heavy Rain and Flooding: Latest Forecast
summary: On Monday, parts of New Jersey were underwater, downed trees were blocking roads in Connecticut and there was a flash flood warning in New York City.

title: Heat waves are taking a big toll on chickens
summary: The birds are particularly vulnerable to high temperatures, and France’s torrid June took a heavy toll.

title: Extreme Heat Hits Europe for the Third Time This Year
summary: It is continent’s third spell of scorching weather since May. Europe is the world’s fastest-warming continent.

title: Inside Bath & Body Works’ Fragrance Factory
summary: “Japanese Cherry Blossom” has brought in $1.5 billion over the last 20 years. Can the company’s close-knit network of suppliers come up with the next big thing and improve sales?

title: The Liberal American Jew’s Dilemma
summary: Over the years, I have moved toward the way of being Jewish that my father rejected.

title: Bring Back the SAT, California
summary: Under the test-blind policy, more students are thrown into classes for which they are unprepared.
